questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 4 and direction 0° (pointing right along the x-axis).
questions.hint: Direction 0° points straight right. Keep y = 0 and move 4 units along the positive x-axis.
questions.explanation: Magnitude 4 at 0° lands the tip at ⟨4, 0⟩: 4 units right and 0 units up. The first number tells you the horizontal movement and the second the vertical movement, so a vector pointing straight right has a y-component of 0.

questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 3 and direction 270° (pointing straight down).
questions.hint: Direction 270° points straight down. Keep x = 0 and move 3 units along the negative y-axis.
questions.explanation: Magnitude 3 at 270° lands the tip at ⟨0, −3⟩: 0 units horizontally and 3 units down. Pointing straight down means there is no left/right movement (x = 0) and the vertical component is negative.

questions.prompt: Draw a vector with magnitude 2√2 (about 2.8) and direction 225° (down and to the left).
questions.hint: At 225° the horizontal and vertical parts are equal, and both point in the negative direction. A magnitude of 2√2 splits into 2 left and 2 down.
questions.explanation: At 225° the tip moves equally left and down, so the components are equal and both negative. Magnitude 2√2 gives ⟨−2, −2⟩, since √((−2)² + (−2)²) = √8 = 2√2.
